title: A Leela Of Her Own

jokes.text: Put down the binoculars. The wall of that strip club isn't gonna collapse twice in one day.
jokes.setup: Character telling someone to put down binoculars
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're watching for a strip club wall to collapse again
jokes.explanation: Strong opener with absurd specificity and deadpan delivery. The casual assumption it won't collapse 'twice in one day' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I know. I accept that. Now I'm interested in that new pizza parlor.
jokes.setup: Response to being told to stop watching for building collapse
jokes.punchline: Casually accepts this and moves to next voyeuristic target
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Bender's shameless voyeurism and quick adaptation to new opportunities.

jokes.text: Makes me pine for my days as a delivery boy. 'Here's your pizza,' I'd say. 'I didn't order any,' they'd say. Then off to my next adventure.
jokes.setup: Fry nostalgically remembers being a delivery boy
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was terrible at it - delivering to wrong addresses
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry characterization - he fondly remembers being incompetent at his job. The cheerful delivery of professional failure is very funny.

jokes.text: That story stunk. Hand me the binoculars.
jokes.setup: Fry tells his nostalgic delivery story
jokes.punchline: Bender dismisses it brutally and returns to voyeurism
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - blunt dismissal of friends and return to selfish interests. Economical and in character.

jokes.text: The owner is from Signas-Five. Signoids? On our block? They should go back where they came from.
jokes.setup: Learning about alien pizza shop owners
jokes.punchline: Immediate xenophobic reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard xenophobia satire - functional but predictable. Sets up the immigrant storyline.

jokes.text: Society's never gonna make progress... Until we learn to pretend to like each other. Let's make these hideous strangers feel welcome.
jokes.setup: Professor making a statement about social progress
jokes.punchline: Reveals his idea of tolerance is 'pretending' and calls them 'hideous strangers'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Professor line - starts noble, reveals his complete misunderstanding of tolerance. Perfect character writing with excellent structure.

jokes.text: You sure about these chairs? Guidebook says human legs bend down at knee.
jokes.setup: Alien restaurant owner consulting guidebook about humans
jokes.punchline: Has to look up basic human anatomy for furniture
jokes.explanation: Great alien perspective humor - they're so foreign they need a manual for basic human physiology. Well-crafted absurdity.

jokes.text: It's okay, Mama. Anyone complains, I bend legs up for free.
jokes.setup: Son reassuring mother about the chair situation
jokes.punchline: Offers to bend customer legs instead of chairs
jokes.explanation: Darkly absurd solution that shows alien misunderstanding of customer service. Economical and unexpected.

jokes.text: Welcome to Earth Pizza Store. Here, sit. I bend the knees for you.
jokes.setup: Owner greeting customers and offering seating
jokes.punchline: Actually follows through on bending their legs
jokes.explanation: Good callback that delivers on the setup. Physical comedy implied by the dialogue response.

jokes.text: What kind of pizza goes in you?
jokes.setup: Taking pizza order from customers
jokes.punchline: Phrasing implies pizza goes 'in' the customer rather than 'to' them
jokes.explanation: Alien language confusion creates unintentionally suggestive phrasing. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Silt? Asbestos? We got guano, very fresh.
jokes.setup: Listing pizza toppings
jokes.punchline: All toppings are toxic/inedible substances, proudly advertised as 'very fresh'
jokes.explanation: Escalating absurdity of inedible 'toppings' with the pride in freshness of bat droppings. Well-structured list joke.

jokes.text: Have any food? Here, try a deep-Dish pizza. Contains four kinds of things.
jokes.setup: Customer reasonably asking for actual food
jokes.punchline: Pizza contains vague 'four kinds of things' rather than ingredients
jokes.explanation: Good alien ignorance of food preparation. The vagueness of 'four kinds of things' is appropriately unsettling.

jokes.text: This tastes like vomit. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Customer tasting the terrible pizza
jokes.punchline: Thanks them for something that tastes like vomit
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection with polite response to awful experience. Economical and effective.

jokes.text: Actually, I mean to offend a little. This is awful.
jokes.setup: Correcting the previous customer's politeness
jokes.punchline: Clarifies that she actually wants to be rude about how bad it is
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leela - direct, honest, and willing to be blunt when needed. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: We come to Earth to start new life, raise fat, spoiled Earth kids...
jokes.setup: Explaining their immigrant dreams
jokes.punchline: Aspires specifically to raising 'fat, spoiled' children as the ideal
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of American Dream stereotypes - they want the negative aspects as ideals.

jokes.text: Hang many underwear from Earth clothesline, live Earthican dream.
jokes.setup: Continuing to describe their Earth dreams
jokes.punchline: Hanging underwear on clotheslines is seen as peak Earth living
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and mundane detail elevated to immigrant dream status. Good alien perspective humor.

jokes.text: As soon as I stop blasting puke... I'm gonna teach you to sell pizza.
jokes.setup: Fry offering to help after eating the terrible pizza
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions vomiting while making generous offer
jokes.explanation: Good Fry characterization - helpful despite personal discomfort. The casual vomit mention while being generous is very funny.

jokes.text: You save us, sir. If we cannot make Earth Pizza, our dream will die.
jokes.setup: Dramatic declaration about their dreams dying
jokes.punchline: Sets up the dark rat wine punchline
jokes.explanation: Straightforward setup line - functional but not particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: Just like rats we crushed to make the wine.
jokes.setup: Talking about dreams dying
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals they make wine from crushed rats
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - connects metaphor to literal horror. Perfect economy and unexpected revelation that reframes their cooking entirely.

jokes.text: For starters, use quality ingredients. Case in point? No more live bees.
jokes.setup: Fry giving basic cooking advice
jokes.punchline: Has to specify not to use live bees as ingredient
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor - implies they were using live bees before. Well-structured advice joke.

jokes.text: When you put pizzas in the oven, don't get in with them.
jokes.setup: More basic cooking advice from Fry
jokes.punchline: Has to tell them not to climb in the oven with the pizza
jokes.explanation: Escalating absurdity of basic advice - implies dangerous ignorance. Economical and visual.

jokes.text: Hey, ocupado.
jokes.setup: Response to advice about not getting in oven
jokes.punchline: Speaks from inside the oven, indicating they didn't follow the advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback - they're literally in the oven as Fry is telling them not to be. Great economy and timing.

jokes.text: Families need a TV so they don't have to talk to each other.
jokes.setup: Explaining the importance of big-screen TVs
jokes.punchline: TV exists to prevent family communication
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary delivered matter-of-factly. Classic cynical observation about American family life.

jokes.text: Mets lose again. I haven't seen play this bad since the days of Bob Uecker.
jokes.setup: Sports announcer commenting on bad play
jokes.punchline: References Bob Uecker as example of bad play
jokes.explanation: Setup for the next joke - functional but not particularly strong on its own.

jokes.text: This is Bob Uecker saying thanks for watching.
jokes.setup: Announcer just criticized Bob Uecker's play
jokes.punchline: Bob Uecker himself is doing the broadcast
jokes.explanation: Great meta joke - the announcer criticizing Uecker IS Uecker. Perfect timing and self-aware humor.

jokes.text: Mets? Shortstop? Pinching the hitter? I don't understand this Blernsball.
jokes.setup: Alien trying to understand baseball terminology
jokes.punchline: Misunderstands 'pinch hitting' as literal pinching
jokes.explanation: Good alien confusion humor with baseball terminology. Sets up the blernsball premise nicely.

jokes.text: Go, team. Beat those no-Good Signoids.
jokes.setup: Crowd cheering for Planet Express team
jokes.punchline: Racist cheering against the aliens they just helped
jokes.explanation: Shows the casual racism persists despite helping them. Functional social commentary but predictable.

jokes.text: Ah, this is why I love Earth. Beautiful star-Brightened day, friendly Blernsball game with chum pals.
jokes.setup: Alien expressing love for Earth experience
jokes.punchline: Oblivious to the racist crowd chanting against them
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - alien's innocent joy contrasted with crowd hostility he can't hear.

jokes.text: No batter, no batter. No batter anymore. Take your base.
jokes.setup: Traditional 'no batter' chant from catcher
jokes.punchline: Evolves to 'no batter anymore' after Leela injures them
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation joke - traditional baseball chant becomes darkly literal. Perfect economy and structure.

jokes.text: Yeah. Me too, now.
jokes.setup: Leela mentions her depth perception problems
jokes.punchline: Injured batter now has the same problem from being hit
jokes.explanation: Dark and efficient callback - injury creates shared disability. Excellent economy with just three words.

jokes.text: Hey, batter, batter, batter, batter. Hey, duck.
jokes.setup: Traditional batting encouragement chant
jokes.punchline: Switches to warning the batter to duck
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from encouragement to survival warning. Shows Leela's dangerous pitching.

jokes.text: Please, I want to live. Can I use bat to protect head? Apparently not.
jokes.setup: Batter pleading for safety
jokes.punchline: Tries to use bat as shield, fails immediately
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of danger with failed defensive attempt. The quick 'apparently not' is well-timed.

jokes.text: Check out the one-Eyed bean machine.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Leela's pitching performance
jokes.punchline: Nicknames her 'bean machine' for hitting batters
jokes.explanation: Clever nickname combining her physical trait with her 'skill'. Good wordplay with baseball terminology.

jokes.text: Hooray, I make a score point.
jokes.setup: Alien celebrating after being hit by pitch
jokes.punchline: Calls it a 'score point' instead of run/point
jokes.explanation: Good alien confusion with sports terminology. Shows their innocent joy despite being injured.

jokes.text: You beaned a run in. Better let me pitch. But I've got a no-Hitter going. You're right. Here.
jokes.setup: Teammate wants to replace Leela's pitching
jokes.punchline: Leela proud of 'no-hitter' which is actually hitting every batter
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misunderstanding of baseball terminology - she thinks 'no-hitter' means hitting no balls rather than allowing no hits. Perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: Now I'm the sleaze bag who owns the New New York Mets.
jokes.setup: Character introducing himself after previous description
jokes.punchline: Proudly accepts 'sleaze bag' title with new team ownership
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing shameless acceptance of negative description. Sets up sports storyline.

jokes.text: The Mets? Those bums are worse than me.
jokes.setup: Learning about the Mets team
jokes.punchline: Considers them worse than herself despite her terrible pitching
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the sports storyline with Leela's overconfidence despite obvious lack of skill.

jokes.text: You mean, I'd be the first woman ever to play major league blernsball?
jokes.setup: Owner explaining the publicity stunt aspect
jokes.punchline: Leela focuses on the historic aspect, ignoring the 'stunt' part
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag of this phrase being repeated. Shows Leela's idealistic interpretation.

jokes.text: It'd be a publicity stunt. A one-Eyed lady might bring out the freak show crowd. Wow, the first woman ever to play major league blernsball.
jokes.setup: Owner being brutally honest about exploiting her
jokes.punchline: Leela completely ignores the insult and focuses on the positive
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Leela's selective hearing when it comes to achieving her dreams.

jokes.text: Like a sexy Yogi Berra. Why is your number 7/8?
jokes.setup: Commenting on how Leela looks in uniform
jokes.punchline: Her jersey number is a fraction
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist detail - fractional jersey numbers suggest she's not quite a full player.

jokes.text: The whole numbers have been retired.
jokes.setup: Explaining the fractional number
jokes.punchline: ALL whole numbers are retired, leaving only fractions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - implies the team has retired so many numbers they've run out of integers. Perfect economy and originality.

jokes.text: Can I pat you on the butt? I'm a professional athlete. So go ahead. Now I'm too nervous.
jokes.setup: Amy asking permission for traditional athlete congratulation
jokes.punchline: Gets permission but becomes too nervous to follow through
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Amy showing her awkwardness when permission is actually granted.

jokes.text: The Swedes have already turned this one into a 'laffer'.
jokes.setup: Sports announcer commenting on the game situation
jokes.punchline: References Swedish comedy films with wordplay on 'laugher'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but relatively obscure reference. Shows the game is so bad it's becoming entertainment.

jokes.text: And that's with two 'F's.' The crowd is pouring out L.A. -Style.
jokes.setup: Explaining the spelling of 'laffer'
jokes.punchline: Crowd leaving early like stereotypical LA sports fans
jokes.explanation: Follows up the previous joke with LA sports fan stereotype. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: I already put in the circus clown. But he bunted. Clowns are only funny when they swing away.
jokes.setup: Manager explaining why the clown didn't work
jokes.punchline: Clown failed by playing smart baseball instead of being entertaining
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist logic about clown entertainment value vs. baseball strategy. Shows the team's desperation.

jokes.text: A one-Eyed woman.
jokes.setup: Formal introduction of Leela as pitcher
jokes.punchline: Blunt, clinical description after the fanfare
jokes.explanation: Good deflating moment after the formal announcement. The clinical tone contrasts with the historic moment.

jokes.text: Come on, throw like a girl. Go, Leela. Too much eyeliner.
jokes.setup: Crowd trying to encourage and insult simultaneously
jokes.punchline: Criticizes her makeup while she's about to pitch
jokes.explanation: Shows the mixed/confused crowd reaction to female athlete. The makeup criticism is appropriately shallow.

jokes.text: An inauspicious continuation for the career of the first woman blernsballer.
jokes.setup: Following up 'inauspicious start' from previous pitch
jokes.punchline: 'Continuation' suggests ongoing failure pattern
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the announcer's professional understatement as failure continues.

jokes.text: Bean! Bean! Bean! Bean! Bean...
jokes.setup: Crowd chanting about Leela's tendency to hit batters
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic chanting for violence/failure
jokes.explanation: Shows crowd enjoying the spectacle of failure. Simple but effective crowd dynamics.

jokes.text: You got more publicity than a cowboy in a shark tank. Poor Tex. He was quite a shark.
jokes.setup: Comparison to cowboy in shark tank getting publicity
jokes.punchline: Reveals Tex was actually skilled (a 'shark') making it tragic
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay joke with 'shark' having double meaning. The follow-up makes it darker and funnier.

jokes.text: You put a one and two zeros in front of that, or we pass. Deal. That's great. How much did you get me? One hundred dollars!
jokes.setup: Bender negotiating what sounds like a big deal
jokes.punchline: His 'one and two zeros' actually made it smaller (100 vs 1000+)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bender moment - his math incompetence ruins the negotiation. Perfect character integration with great misdirection.

jokes.text: As a pitcher, I serve up plenty of bean balls. So I know good beans when I see them. Bean-Bay Beans! They're the beaniest!
jokes.setup: Commercial using Leela's baseball 'skill'
jokes.punchline: Turns her failure (hitting batters) into product endorsement qualification
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay connecting baseball 'beans' to actual beans. Shows how her failure becomes marketable.

jokes.text: You put a one and two zeros in front of that, or we pass. Deal. So, what did you get me? A thousand and one pesos.
jokes.setup: Bender using the same negotiation phrase again
jokes.punchline: Gets foreign currency, continues to be a terrible agent
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Bender's terrible negotiating. The foreign currency adds to his incompetence.

jokes.text: To eBay? That's a popular name today. Little 'e,' big 'B'?
jokes.setup: Girl asking for autograph 'to eBay'
jokes.punchline: Leela thinks it's a person's name and asks about capitalization
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference humor with Leela's innocent misunderstanding of the girl's mercenary intentions.

jokes.text: Five bucks an autograph, 200 fans, add a one and two zeros, we got a wad.
jokes.setup: Bender calculating autograph money
jokes.punchline: Uses his faulty math formula again incorrectly
jokes.explanation: Running gag callback showing Bender's consistent poor math

jokes.text: A role model for how to stink. What? Who are you?
jokes.setup: Leela talking about being a role model
jokes.punchline: Jackie brutally reframes it as modeling failure
jokes.explanation: Sharp character moment introducing Jackie as the reality check to Leela's delusions.

jokes.text: You are! What about that little girl you visited in the hospital who died?
jokes.setup: Fry trying to cheer up Leela about being heroic
jokes.punchline: The example he gives involves a child dying
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Fry moment - tries to be supportive but gives the worst possible example. Perfect character writing.

jokes.text: That's statistically impossible. In 77 innings, you haven't gotten one out.
jokes.setup: Leela vowing to be the best player ever
jokes.punchline: Professor provides brutal statistical reality
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor moment - crushes dreams with mathematics. The specific stat makes it worse.

jokes.text: Then I have a new vow. I solemnly swear I will become not the worst blernsball player of all time!
jokes.setup: Leela adjusting her goals after hearing the statistics
jokes.punchline: Lowers her ambition to just 'not the worst ever'
jokes.explanation: Great character development showing Leela's pragmatic goal adjustment. The formal vow language makes it funnier.

jokes.text: When will man learn that all races are equally inferior to robots?
jokes.setup: Looking at display of players who broke color barriers
jokes.punchline: Bender's typical robot supremacist take
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender line - takes civil rights moment and makes it about robot superiority. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: He once struck out when his tongue stuck to a cold bat.
jokes.setup: Describing bad player Hank Aaron XXIV
jokes.punchline: Struck out due to tongue freezing to bat
jokes.explanation: Great absurd failure scenario. Visual and specific enough to be memorable.

jokes.text: He tried to catch with an oven mitt! It's a crummy hologram. I'm not a hologram. Hank Aaron the 24th.
jokes.setup: More examples of terrible play, dismissing him as hologram
jokes.punchline: He's actually real and present, making the insults worse
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - the insults become personal when he reveals he's real. Escalates the embarrassment.

jokes.text: My batting average was low. Low? It was zero! You went your whole career without getting a hit.
jokes.setup: Hank understating his poor performance
jokes.punchline: Guide reveals the full extent - literally never got a hit
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from 'low' to the worst possible batting average. Shows the scale of failure.

jokes.text: Leela beats that. She pitched without ever getting an out!
jokes.setup: Hank's record for worst hitting
jokes.punchline: Leela's pitching failure is even more comprehensive
jokes.explanation: Establishes Leela as potentially worse than the worst player ever. Sets up her training motivation.

jokes.text: Wade Boggs, goes down smooth.
jokes.setup: Looking at Wade Boggs in hall of fame
jokes.punchline: Treats him like an alcoholic beverage advertisement
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay on Wade Boggs' name connecting to alcohol. Somewhat random but fits character's confusion.

jokes.text: I can't help you play better than Tiny lota. He was great!
jokes.setup: Leela asking to be less pathetic than him
jokes.punchline: Misunderstands 'tiny iota' as player name 'Tiny Iota' who was apparently good
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay misunderstanding. Creates fictional great player from common expression.

jokes.text: You mean on the ball? Who's in the hall of fame?
jokes.setup: Leela correcting Hank's advice to keep eye 'off' the ball
jokes.punchline: Hank dismisses her correction with his credentials
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - terrible player using hall of fame status to defend bad advice.

jokes.text: Strike! Man, you did it, Leela! I didn't hit the batter!
jokes.setup: Leela finally throws a strike
jokes.punchline: She celebrates not hitting the batter rather than throwing a strike
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - shows how low her expectations have become. The wrong celebration focus is very funny.

jokes.text: You opened a franchise! Yes. Our biggest seller is Leela's Bean Pizza!
jokes.setup: Learning about the Signoids' business success
jokes.punchline: They've capitalized on Leela's 'bean ball' reputation for pizza marketing
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the bean ball theme, showing how they've monetized her failure.

jokes.text: Live hornets. We smoosh them right into dough.
jokes.setup: Revealing their 'ancient secret' for fizzy crust
jokes.punchline: They literally put live stinging insects in the food
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the live bees advice. Hornets are even worse, showing they learned nothing.

jokes.text: I don't care if it's horse manure. That's good.
jokes.setup: Learning the pizza contains live hornets
jokes.punchline: Doesn't care about the disgusting ingredients because it tastes good
jokes.explanation: Shows the absurd standards people will accept for good taste. The horse manure comparison is appropriately gross.

jokes.text: No! We come to Earth to make pizza, not money! No, Blek! Other way around.
jokes.setup: Father nobly refusing money for their dream
jokes.punchline: Wife immediately corrects him - they want money, not pizza
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic showing wife as the practical one. Quick correction makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Fans, Boston's turning the last game into a real 'squeeeker'.
jokes.setup: Announcing close game situation
jokes.punchline: Another made-up word with explanation of spelling
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag of announcer's made-up words. Functional but getting repetitive.

jokes.text: I repeat. Isn't there a man on...
jokes.setup: Manager asking if there's a man who can get an out
jokes.punchline: Realizes Leela volunteered and tries to restate for men only
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing manager's sexist assumptions

jokes.text: I've never seen anything this weird and I've seen Mr. Belvedere naked!
jokes.setup: Describing how weird the female vs female matchup is
jokes.punchline: Compares it to seeing naked 80s TV character
jokes.explanation: Random pop culture reference escalation. Weird enough to be memorable but lacks deeper craft.

jokes.text: She puts on her sports bra one arm at a time.
jokes.setup: Leela psyching herself up about facing another player
jokes.punchline: Adapts 'puts pants on one leg at a time' saying to sports bra
jokes.explanation: Good adaptation of common saying to female athlete context. Shows Leela trying to relate.

jokes.text: This moment is brought to you by Month Old Franks... The hot dogs with experience!
jokes.setup: Commercial break during tense moment
jokes.punchline: Advertises month-old hot dogs as having 'experience'
jokes.explanation: Good absurd advertising parody. Turns spoiled food into a selling point with 'experience.'

jokes.text: She's taught the world that being a bum knows no gender.
jokes.setup: Summing up Leela's historic impact
jokes.punchline: Her legacy is proving women can fail just as badly as men
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of inspirational sports moment. Her 'achievement' is proving equal opportunity failure.

jokes.text: You were so awful that women set out to prove they don't stink as bad as you.
jokes.setup: Explaining how Leela was inspirational after all
jokes.punchline: Her inspiration came from being such a bad example
jokes.explanation: Perfect backhanded compliment that actually makes logical sense. Finds the silver lining in complete failure.

jokes.text: You're still the worst football player of all time. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Kid catching Leela's tossed ball
jokes.punchline: Calls her worst football player, she accepts it casually
jokes.explanation: Good ending showing Leela's acceptance of her limitations. The casual 'yeah' shows growth/resignation.

tmdb_overview: Leela endeavors to become the first female blernsball player, but her lack of depth perception hinders her skills. Nevertheless, she becomes the pitcher for the New New York Mets, purely for her novelty value.
